name: MITRE.LIN.T1529.RULE System Shutdown/Reboot
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

# REDHAT/CENTOS
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/sbin/reboot -k siem-reboot
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/sbin/init -k siem-reboot
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/sbin/poweroff -k siem-reboot
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/sbin/shutdow -k siem-reboot

# DEBIAN/UBUNTU
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/reboot -k siem-reboot
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/init -k siem-reboot
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/poweroff -k siem-reboot
-a exit,always -F arch=b64 -S execve -F path=/usr/sbin/shutdow -k siem-reboot

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-reboot
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1529.RULE System Shutdown/Reboot on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-reboot
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list:
Event description: Adversaries may attempt to shutdown/reboot a system after impacting it in other ways, such as Disk Structure Wipe or Inhibit System Recovery, to hasten the intended effects on system availability.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1529/
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
